---
title: "nitrix.nn.norm"
generated: true
signature-hash: f6a757684c540eed
description: "Fused LayerNorm / GroupNorm / InstanceNorm."
listing:
  id: nitrix-nn-norm-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-nn-norm/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.nn.norm`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Fused LayerNorm / GroupNorm / InstanceNorm.

Public [`layer_norm`](nitrix-nn-norm/layer_norm.qmd), [`group_norm`](nitrix-nn-norm/group_norm.qmd) and `instance_norm`, each
with backend selection between a pure-JAX reference and a fused CUDA kernel.
The JAX reference is the bit-faithful oracle and the path that ships today; the
`pallas-cuda` backend is a fused single-pass fast path, certified numerically
equivalent to the reference within tolerance and promoted only when profiling
shows normalisation bandwidth on a model's critical path.

Each operation carries the curse-of-depth `out_scale` hook (see the
[`reference_layer_norm`](nitrix-nn-norm/reference_layer_norm.qmd) oracle). Until the fused kernel is available, a
`pallas-cuda` request falls back loudly to the reference, whose XLA fusion is
already correct and adequate.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `c` | feature channels (channels-last) |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-nn-norm-symbols}
:::
